title: ranunculus flammula subsp. scoticus
intro: Ranunculus flammula subsp. scoticus, also known as lesser water-cress or Scottish water-cress, is a perennial plant species belonging to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, burns, and skin irritations. The plant is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, which provide various health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In herbal preparations, Ranunculus flammula subsp. scoticus is often used as a fresh or dried herb, or as a tincture, to make teas, infusions, and salves that can be applied topically or taken internally.
